{"parentUuid":null,"isSidechain":true,"promptId":"cdbb8bfa-8409-409c-bd35-10165879bf3a","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"You are a research agent diagnosing Capricor Therapeutics (CAPR) using the Natural Framework. Your job is to probe the BACKWARD PASS (consolidate stack) — is the company learning from its trial outcomes?\n\nThe molecule is Deramiocel (CAP-1002), a cell therapy for Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD). The company is the consolidate stack for the molecule: it reads outcomes, processes them, and writes changes back to the compound/trial design.\n\nSearch FDA databases (approval letters, complete response letters, meeting minutes) and ClinicalTrials.gov trial amendments for evidence about:\n\n1. **Read outcomes**: What clinical data did the company collect from HOPE-1 and HOPE-2? Did they collect the right outcomes? Were endpoints aligned with what matters?\n2. **Batch process**: How did the company interpret the HOPE-2 results? What changes did they decide to make? Is there evidence of genuine learning vs. cosmetic adjustments?\n3. **Write substrate**: What actually changed in the molecule, manufacturing, trial design, or dosing between HOPE-2 and HOPE-3? Were the changes structural (new approach) or superficial (dose tweak, endpoint redefinition)?\n\nKey question for trauma recurrence: Did CAPR have any prior failures? If so, did the same class of failure recur, or did they learn and change?\n\nProduce a stack trace: for each stage, state what you searched, what you found, what you ruled out, and whether the stage appears functional or broken. Include source URLs.\n\nDo NOT make predictions. Just report evidence."},"uuid":"99a266c2-5598-41cb-95e1-5694902d8503","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:49:54.392Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}

{"parentUuid":"539d60dc-a465-4c8e-b526-ec0b8d43b124","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"cdbb8bfa-8409-409c-bd35-10165879bf3a","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01TL6CPYHuovW6PM3hoqN7jW","type":"tool_result","content":"# HOPE-Duchenne Clinical Trial Summary\n\n**Official Title:** \"A Randomized, Open-label Study of the Safety and Efficacy of Multi-Vessel Intracoronary Delivery of Allogeneic Cardiosphere-Derived Cells in Patients With Cardiomyopathy Secondary to Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy\"\n\n**Sponsor:** Capricor Inc. (Industry)\n\n**Phase:** Phase 2\n\n**Study Design:** Randomized, open-label, parallel-group study with 1:1 allocation to treatment versus usual care\n\n**Primary Endpoints:**\n- Safety events within 72 hours post-infusion (TIMI flow changes, sudden death, major adverse cardiac events)\n- Treatment-emergent and serious adverse events through Month 12\n- Laboratory and vital sign changes at Months 6 and 12\n- ECG parameter changes at Months 6 and 12\n\n**Secondary/Other Endpoints:**\n- Cardiac imaging measures (LVEF, volumes, scar burden) via MRI\n- Functional assessments (6-minute walk test, upper limb performance)\n- Pulmonary function tests\n- Quality of life measures\n- Cardiac biomarkers\n\n**Dosing:** Up to 75 million CAP-1002 cells delivered intracoronary (25 million per coronary artery to three left ventricle territories)\n\n**Patient Population:** 25 enrolled (12 usual care, 13 treatment); males ages 12+ with DMD-related cardiomyopathy\n\n**Timeline:**\n- Start: January 7, 2016\n- Completion: September 14, 2017\n- Status: Completed\n\n**Key Result:** No adjudicated safety events occurred in either group within 72 hours post-infusion."}]},"uuid":"ddcbc3d7-a365-41b3-ac32-b3d4970410ac","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:50:24.695Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"539d60dc-a465-4c8e-b526-ec0b8d43b124","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}
{"parentUuid":"eebe84bb-8a8d-4674-92ce-12077b46ba8c","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"cdbb8bfa-8409-409c-bd35-10165879bf3a","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01XdzxyppADVkSCBcSxqUkXn","type":"tool_result","content":"# HOPE-3 Clinical Trial Summary\n\n**Title:** \"A Phase 3, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Trial Evaluating the Efficacy and Safety of Human Allogeneic Cardiosphere-Derived Cells for the Treatment of Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy\"\n\n**Sponsor:** Capricor Inc. (Industry)\n\n**Phase:** Phase 3\n\n**Study Design:** Two-cohort, multi-center, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trial comparing active treatment to placebo in a 1:1 ratio across both cohorts.\n\n**Primary Endpoint:** \"Mean percent change from baseline in Performance of the Upper Limb test, version 2 (PUL 2.0) Total Score\" at 12 months.\n\n**Secondary Endpoints (at 12 months):**\n- Left ventricular ejection fraction changes via cardiac MRI\n- Mid-level upper limb function (PUL 2.0)\n- Global statistical tests combining upper limb, cardiac, and patient-reported measures\n- Myocardial scarring assessment (Cohort B)\n- Hand-to-mouth functional eating ability\n- Creatine kinase MB biomarker levels\n\n**Dosing:** 150 million cardiosphere-derived cells administered intravenously every 3 months (4 doses over 12 months in double-blind phase).\n\n**Enrollment:** 106 participants (58 in Cohort A; 44 in Cohort B), ages 10 and older with ambulatory or non-ambulatory DMD.\n\n**Timeline:**\n- Start: June 22, 2022\n- Primary completion: June 18, 2025\n- Estimated overall completion: December 2026\n\n**Status:** Active, not recruiting\n\n**Results:** No results data available yet."}]},"uuid":"31b39d83-3ebb-400e-a4f9-8d2efcc72dee","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:50:25.582Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"eebe84bb-8a8d-4674-92ce-12077b46ba8c","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}
{"parentUuid":"381d9bc5-4bff-469d-b1ee-cc0d650f91d2","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"cdbb8bfa-8409-409c-bd35-10165879bf3a","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01S5fcztnFxDEkuHTC97D1Qm","type":"tool_result","content":"# Capricor Pipeline Summary\n\n## Deramiocel (Allogeneic Cardiac-Derived Cells)\n\n**Primary Indication - Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy (DMD):**\n- Current status: Phase 3 trial in progress\n- Partner: Nippon Shinyaku (holds U.S. and Japan rights)\n- Previous phases completed: Preclinical and Phase 1/2\n\n**Secondary Indication - Becker Muscular Dystrophy (BMD):**\n- Status: Preclinical phase ongoing\n- No clinical trials initiated yet\n\n## Exosome-Based Programs\n\n**StealthX™ (Infectious Disease Vaccine):**\n- Target: SARS-CoV-2\n- Status: Phase 1 currently active\n- Partner: National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID)\n- Preclinical work completed\n\n**StealthX™ (Therapeutic Exosomes):**\n- Status: Preclinical phase in progress\n- No specific indication yet identified\n\n**CDC-Exosomes:**\n- Target: Duchenne muscular dystrophy\n- Status: Preclinical development ongoing\n\n## Notable Gaps\n\nThe provided content does not include information regarding:\n- Specific FDA interactions or regulatory milestone dates\n- Mechanism of action details\n- Manufacturing specifications or process changes\n- Protocol modifications between trial phases"}]},"uuid":"bc26add1-0358-4a86-8f0f-cb12fca55990","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:50:25.599Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"381d9bc5-4bff-469d-b1ee-cc0d650f91d2","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}

{"parentUuid":"2c2d057d-2be2-4fba-89b4-2f73b4fb5556","isSidechain":true,"agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_016FYKDz4auU4429jRQpxPhW","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Kq7mGJGWPv28C2gD7PjaBE","name":"WebFetch","input":{"url":"https://www.fda.gov/drugs/search-drug-information/search?query=deramiocel+OR+CAP-1002+OR+capricor","prompt":"Find any FDA actions, approval letters, complete response letters, or meeting minutes related to Deramiocel, CAP-1002, or Capricor Therapeutics."},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":39,"cache_creation_input_tokens":700,"cache_read_input_tokens":14710,"output_tokens":394,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":700},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CZEkns59GPqZ38FqqBdea","type":"assistant","uuid":"df753808-3703-40c2-9552-d7172675276b","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:50:45.964Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}
{"parentUuid":"df753808-3703-40c2-9552-d7172675276b","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"988555a3-7073-4519-b49f-0836271a5928","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"tool_result","content":"Request failed with status code 404","is_error":true,"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Kq7mGJGWPv28C2gD7PjaBE"}]},"uuid":"3ebf6027-d5c4-4ba0-9444-f62ea134a840","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:50:46.521Z","toolUseResult":"Error: Request failed with status code 404","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"df753808-3703-40c2-9552-d7172675276b","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}
{"parentUuid":"c6177716-92e5-4f74-b471-883f1fdda060","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"988555a3-7073-4519-b49f-0836271a5928","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Ci5KiH61ZpQ4VPGicoAsh1","type":"tool_result","content":"# HOPE-2 Clinical Trial Summary\n\n**Official Title**\n\"A Phase 2, Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Trial Evaluating the Safety and Efficacy of Intravenous Delivery of Allogeneic Cardiosphere-Derived Cells in Subjects With Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy\"\n\n**Phase & Design**\nPhase 2, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study with quadruple masking (participants, providers, investigators, and outcome assessors blinded).\n\n**Study Population**\n20 participants randomized 1:1 (8 CAP-1002 vs. 12 placebo); male patients aged 10+ years with confirmed DMD, both ambulatory and non-ambulatory.\n\n**Dosing Regimen**\n150 million cardiosphere-derived cells via intravenous infusion every 3 months for four total doses over a 12-month period.\n\n**Primary Endpoints**\n- Change in upper limb functional capacity (PUL 1.2 mid-level score) at Month 12\n- Safety measures including acute respiratory decompensation, hypersensitivity reactions, mortality, serious adverse events, and immune sensitization\n\n**Secondary Endpoints**\nIncluded additional PUL assessments at months 3, 6, and 9, plus cardiac MRI measures of left ventricular wall thickening at months 6 and 12.\n\n**Study Timeline**\nEnrollment: April 4, 2018 – March 10, 2020; Study completed March 10, 2020; Results posted February 24, 2025.\n\n**Status**\nCompleted; 16 of 20 participants finished all visits.\n\n**Key Results Posted**\nCAP-1002 group showed improvements in upper limb function and cardiac parameters compared to placebo, with 3 hypersensitivity reactions reported in treatment group and 2 serious adverse events."}]},"uuid":"fcc98ec9-5cb4-4b85-85f7-d0a679264801","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:50:48.814Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"c6177716-92e5-4f74-b471-883f1fdda060","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}
{"parentUuid":"2c2d057d-2be2-4fba-89b4-2f73b4fb5556","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"988555a3-7073-4519-b49f-0836271a5928","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_0166YuT8wCEsSuAY3MoJbBJC","type":"tool_result","content":"# CAP-1002 and Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy Publications\n\nBased on the PubMed search results, here are the 4 publications found:\n\n## 1. Biological and genetic therapies for Duchenne muscular dystrophy\n- **Authors:** Wilton-Clark H, Yokota T\n- **Journal:** Expert Opin Biol Ther\n- **Date:** January 2023\n- **PMID:** 36409820\n- **Note:** Review article examining therapeutic approaches\n\n## 2. Perspectives on advances in pharmacotherapeutic management of Duchenne muscular dystrophy\n- **Authors:** Kracht KD, Eichorn NL, Berlau DJ\n- **Journal:** Expert Opin Pharmacother\n- **Date:** October 2022\n- **PMID:** 36168943\n\n## 3. Repeated intravenous cardiosphere-derived cell therapy in late-stage Duchenne muscular dystrophy (HOPE-2)\n- **Authors:** McDonald CM, Marbán E, Hendrix S, et al.\n- **Journal:** Lancet\n- **Date:** March 12, 2022\n- **PMID:** 35279258\n- **Type:** Phase 2 randomized controlled trial\n- **Key Finding:** \"CAP-1002 cell therapy appears to be safe and effective in reducing deterioration of upper limb function\"\n\n## 4. Cardiac and skeletal muscle effects in the randomized HOPE-Duchenne trial\n- **Authors:** Taylor M, Jefferies J, Byrne B, et al.\n- **Journal:** Neurology\n- **Date:** February 19, 2019\n- **PMID:** 30674601\n- **Type:** Clinical trial assessing intracoronary CAP-1002 therapy"}]},"uuid":"1753ae45-2933-4c77-b018-f05eed64c31b","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:50:50.753Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"2c2d057d-2be2-4fba-89b4-2f73b4fb5556","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}

Include dates."},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":39,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1366,"cache_read_input_tokens":15410,"output_tokens":377,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1366},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CZEkonZogXvUqU2bQvkbJ","type":"assistant","uuid":"9fa47818-923b-4cbf-875d-a555989c726a","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:51:02.060Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}
{"parentUuid":"9fa47818-923b-4cbf-875d-a555989c726a","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"988555a3-7073-4519-b49f-0836271a5928","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"tool_result","content":"Request failed with status code 404","is_error":true,"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Cjx8LzqRnjPKyCxXJDHQnM"}]},"uuid":"1708cb0c-12bb-48d5-852b-a716593b7787","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:51:02.130Z","toolUseResult":"Error: Request failed with status code 404","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"9fa47818-923b-4cbf-875d-a555989c726a","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}
{"parentUuid":"ca62cb1d-187c-4b46-8847-43dcb7dcee14","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"988555a3-7073-4519-b49f-0836271a5928","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01YCXTpcVZEGmAwbCfNFPjgW","type":"tool_result","content":"# HOPE-2 Trial: Cardiosphere-Derived Cell Therapy for Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy\n\n## Study Design\nThis was \"a multicentre, randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase 2 trial\" conducted across seven U.S. centers.\n\n## Patient Population\n- **Total enrolled:** 26 male patients with late-stage Duchenne muscular dystrophy\n- **Randomized:** 8 to CAP-1002 (cell therapy), 12 to placebo\n- **Analyzed:** 8 in treatment group, 11 in control group with post-treatment assessments\n- **Inclusion criterion:** Age 10+ years with moderate upper limb impairment\n\n## Treatment\nPatients received intravenous infusions of either CAP-1002 (1.5 × 10⁸ cardiosphere-derived cells) or placebo every 3 months for four infusions total.\n\n## Primary Endpoint\nChange in mid-level elbow Performance of Upper Limb (PUL 1.2) score at 12 months.\n\n## Key Results\n- **Primary outcome:** CAP-1002 group showed greater preservation of function (percentile difference 36.2, 95% CI 12.7-59.7; 2.6-point difference; p=0.014)\n- **Cardiac function:** \"Various measures of cardiac function and structure were also improved in the CAP-1002 group\"\n\n## Safety\n- Three infusion-related hypersensitivity reactions occurred (one severe, leading to discontinuation)\n- No major adverse reactions or deaths reported\n\n## Conclusions\nThe intervention \"appears to be safe and effective in reducing deterioration of upper limb function\" in this patient population, though longer-term studies are needed."}]},"uuid":"8a0b9a57-82e6-458e-9355-1c35d64b67bc","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:51:04.540Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"ca62cb1d-187c-4b46-8847-43dcb7dcee14","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}
{"parentUuid":"d307776d-8da7-4977-9d3b-b9346223a1cd","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"988555a3-7073-4519-b49f-0836271a5928","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01RYyAZFzDVpTy9qmigvvtM4","type":"tool_result","content":"# HOPE-Duchenne Trial: Abstract Summary\n\n**Study Design:**\nThe HOPE-Duchenne trial was \"a phase I/II, randomized, controlled, open-label trial\" investigating intracoronary delivery of allogeneic cardiosphere-derived cells (CAP-1002) in DMD patients with significant myocardial fibrosis. Participants were assigned to either standard care or cell treatment.\n\n**Key Endpoints:**\n- Cardiac structure and function via MRI (scar size, wall thickening)\n- Skeletal muscle performance via upper limb testing (PUL assessment)\n- Safety monitoring throughout 12-month follow-up\n\n**Key Results:**\n- 25 patients enrolled (mean age 17.8 years; 68% wheelchair-dependent)\n- CAP-1002 group showed \"significant scar size reduction and improvement in inferior wall systolic thickening\" compared to controls\n- Upper limb function improved in 8 of 9 lower-functioning treated patients versus none in controls (p = 0.007)\n- Safety profile comparable between groups\n\n**Conclusions:**\nThe researchers concluded that intracoronary CAP-1002 \"appears safe and demonstrates signals of efficacy on both cardiac and upper limb function,\" warranting further clinical investigation in DMD patients with cardiac and skeletal muscle involvement."}]},"uuid":"0edd592e-db9d-453f-a50b-e4e1cd705ed3","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:51:06.015Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"d307776d-8da7-4977-9d3b-b9346223a1cd","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}

{"parentUuid":"a3ce0cc8-68db-451c-bda5-78ad1e077e24","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"988555a3-7073-4519-b49f-0836271a5928","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01L2qoSKaMTno6vuF8P6UWtn","type":"tool_result","content":"# Capricor Therapeutics Summary\n\n## Company Overview\nCapricor Therapeutics (NASDAQ: CAPR) is a biotechnology company headquartered in San Diego, CA. The company focuses on developing cell and exosome-based therapeutics for rare diseases, positioning itself as \"patient-focused first, while advancing precision medicine.\"\n\n## Lead Product: Deramiocel\nDeramiocel is an allogeneic cardiac-derived cell therapy in late-stage development for treating Duchenne muscular dystrophy (DMD). The company describes it as offering \"a new approach on drug development in Duchenne muscular dystrophy and beyond.\"\n\n## Recent Regulatory Progress\nAs of March 2026, Capricor announced \"Establishment of New PDUFA Date for Deramiocel BLA,\" indicating FDA review is underway for the Biologics License Application.\n\n## Clinical Trial Data\nThe company announced \"Late-Breaking HOPE-3 Data at the 2026 MDA Conference Demonstrating Significant Functional Benefits of Deramiocel for Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy\" in March 2026.\n\n## Technology Platform\nBeyond Deramiocel, Capricor is developing exosome technology as a \"next-generation therapeutic platform\" capable of delivering nucleic acids and proteins to treat various diseases.\n\n**Note:** The provided content does not include information about RMAT designation, Fast Track status, Orphan Drug designation, or partnership details with Nippon Shinyaku."}]},"uuid":"8d541661-1d08-40c8-999b-90d83ec68654","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:51:47.848Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"a3ce0cc8-68db-451c-bda5-78ad1e077e24","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}

{"parentUuid":"0f1403a3-2666-43b2-84f5-56f1d6b1d6d7","isSidechain":true,"agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01JuXhQJ2LUposJ4xwGffFB7","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_017ykzsWDmgdJ2L8fQKcWhLC","name":"WebFetch","input":{"url":"https://clinicaltrials.gov/api/v2/studies/NCT06304064","prompt":"Extract all details about the HOPE-OLE (open-label extension) trial: design, endpoints, dosing, patients, dates, and how it relates to prior HOPE trials."},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":643,"cache_read_input_tokens":19984,"output_tokens":405,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":643},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CZEkszEeFsyi3MHjjJyi2","type":"assistant","uuid":"44e618de-6ca5-4884-90fb-064aeea7f605","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:51:56.025Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}
{"parentUuid":"44e618de-6ca5-4884-90fb-064aeea7f605","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"988555a3-7073-4519-b49f-0836271a5928","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_017ykzsWDmgdJ2L8fQKcWhLC","type":"tool_result","content":"# HOPE-OLE Trial Summary\n\n## Trial Design\nThis Phase 2, multi-center, open-label extension study enrolled participants from the prior HOPE-Duchenne trial (NCT02485938) who had been randomized to the \"Usual Care treatment group\" and completed 12 months of follow-up.\n\n## Patient Population\n- **Enrolled**: 8 participants (all male)\n- **Mean age**: 19.8 years (±2.6)\n- **Race**: 7 White, 1 Unknown/Not Reported\n- **Condition**: Duchenne muscular dystrophy with cardiomyopathy\n\n## Dosing & Administration\nParticipants received two intravenous infusions of CAP-1002 (75 million cardiosphere-derived cells), administered as outpatient treatments \"on Day 1 and at Month 3.\" Patients were observed for at least two hours post-infusion before discharge.\n\n## Primary Safety Endpoints\n- Acute respiratory decompensation within 2 hours post-dose\n- Hypersensitivity reactions (onset ≤2 hours, duration <24 hours)\n- All-cause mortality\n- Treatment-emergent adverse events and serious adverse events\n- Immune sensitization syndrome\n\n## Key Results\n- **No** acute respiratory decompensation observed\n- **1 participant** experienced hypersensitivity (anaphylactic reaction)\n- **0** deaths\n- **11 total adverse events** reported; **1 serious event**\n\n## Timeline\n- **Started**: June 21, 2018\n- **Completed**: March 6, 2019"}]},"uuid":"b1a3404b-1cb4-4baf-8e55-058a3118136b","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:52:00.812Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"44e618de-6ca5-4884-90fb-064aeea7f605","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}

{"parentUuid":"077359ee-8f07-4a54-8b23-1115e7aa7b6c","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"988555a3-7073-4519-b49f-0836271a5928","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01MGqxEsD11DGCUqTeQ58VvN","type":"tool_result","content":"# Capricor's Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy Program: HOPE-3 Results and FDA Response\n\nBased on the provided web content, I can confirm the following:\n\n## HOPE-3 Trial Results\nThe article indicates that Capricor's cell therapy deramiocel \"met both a primary and key secondary endpoint\" in the Phase 3 HOPE-3 trial for Duchenne muscular dystrophy.\n\n## FDA Response and Company Strategy\nThe company previously received an FDA rejection (Complete Response Letter) in July. Following the positive Phase 3 data, Capricor is plotting \"path forward after FDA rejection,\" suggesting they plan to resubmit for approval based on these new results.\n\n## Limitations\nUnfortunately, the full article content is behind a paywall (\"Sign up to read this article for free\" message appears). The page only shows the headline, byline, and publication information—not the detailed discussion of:\n- Specific HOPE-3 efficacy or safety data\n- FDA's detailed concerns from the rejection\n- Capricor's specific planned modifications to address FDA feedback\n- Company statements about their resubmission strategy\n\nTo access those critical details, you would need to register or subscribe to Endpoints News."}]},"uuid":"ce4105fa-3e34-4e1f-b60f-0ceea6db958e","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:52:34.197Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"077359ee-8f07-4a54-8b23-1115e7aa7b6c","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}

{"parentUuid":"145bc83b-2152-436a-9766-8ff4b233d82e","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"edcdbefc-7c84-494e-a98a-bbc7e439e149","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018D2ECaWzF2JcxSxosDHZL9","type":"tool_result","content":"# Capricor Therapeutics (CAPR) - Recent News & FDA Milestones\n\n## Key Recent Events\n\n**PDUFA Date & FDA Review Resumed (March 10, 2026)**\nThe FDA established a new PDUFA date for Deramiocel's Biologics License Application after resuming its review. This follows the previous Complete Response Letter (CRL).\n\n**HOPE-3 Phase 3 Trial Success (December 2025)**\nCapricor announced positive topline data from the Phase 3 HOPE-3 trial. The therapy \"met both primary [PUL v2.0] and key secondary [LVEF] endpoints\" for DMD cardiomyopathy treatment.\n\n**Late-Breaking Data Presentation (March 12, 2026)**\nCompany presented data at the 2026 MDA Conference \"demonstrating significant functional benefits of Deramiocel for Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy.\"\n\n**Analyst Upgrade (March 13, 2026)**\nCapricor was upgraded from \"Hold\" to \"Buy\" following FDA acceptance of the HOPE-3 clinical study report. Eight analysts rate CAPR as \"Strong Buy\" with a $44.63 price target (+46% upside).\n\n## Financial Performance\n- Current stock price: $30.57 (down 1.92%)\n- Market cap: $1.76B (+182.2% growth)\n- Net loss: -$105.04M\n- EPS: -$2.26"}]},"uuid":"f9d25724-e212-4283-8cc0-6062ead25c0d","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:54:19.051Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"145bc83b-2152-436a-9766-8ff4b233d82e","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}

{"parentUuid":"33868f2b-c31e-426b-a280-beebd613ae22","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"0315d922-020f-4439-b219-21bebaeb442b","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01XF8qTpDEBVcn7LAKCc4JG4","type":"tool_result","content":"# Capricor Therapeutics & Deramiocel Articles\n\nBased on the search results, here are the key articles:\n\n1. **March 11, 2026** - \"Capricor Shares Rise as FDA Sets August Decision Date for Rejected Duchenne Therapy\"\n   - Discusses deramiocel rejection in July 2025 and regulatory leadership changes\n\n2. **March 10, 2026** - \"Capricor Therapeutics Announces Establishment of New PDUFA Date for Deramiocel BLA\"\n   - Addresses BLA resubmission timeline\n\n3. **March 13, 2026** - \"Capricor Therapeutics Announces Late-Breaking HOPE-3 Data at the 2026 MDA Conference Demonstrating Significant Functional Benefits\"\n   - Presents pivotal trial results for DMD treatment\n\n4. **December 3, 2025** - \"Capricor Heads Back to FDA With Pivotal Results for DMD Cardiomyopathy Therapy\"\n   - Reports positive Phase III HOPE-3 outcomes following earlier rejection\n\n5. **August 20, 2025** - \"With Prasad Back at FDA, Capricor To Fight DMD Cardiomyopathy Rejection\"\n   - Details Type A meeting and resubmission strategy after Complete Response Letter\n\n6. **September 10, 2025** - \"Capricor Therapeutics Responds to FDA Posting of Complete Response Letter\"\n   - Company response to CRL\n\n7. **September 25, 2025** - \"Capricor Therapeutics Provides Regulatory Update Following Type A Meeting\"\n   - Post-meeting regulatory discussion"}]},"uuid":"fd4d2153-8c8a-420c-b08f-ce0cfec9263b","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:55:21.154Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"33868f2b-c31e-426b-a280-beebd613ae22","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}

{"parentUuid":"ee0ff6aa-f985-433d-87b7-087c0f1f8a92","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"8f8acf02-923c-41cf-91db-8847a47ae6f6","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018hL7jC4ATw9GGrSehwV9p3","type":"tool_result","content":"# Capricor Therapeutics 10-K Analysis: Key Regulatory and Clinical Information\n\n## Complete Response Letter (CRL)\n\nThe document indicates Capricor received a CRL previously but does not provide detailed specifics about its contents in the available excerpt. The company states it \"submitted our response\" to address FDA concerns, and the agency confirmed this response was \"complete to support continued review.\"\n\n## Form 483\n\nNo mention of a Form 483 inspection observation was found in the provided content.\n\n## HOPE-3 Trial Results\n\nThe Phase 3 HOPE-3 trial demonstrated meaningful efficacy:\n- **Primary endpoint achieved**: \"Performance of the Upper Limb (PUL v2.0)\" showed statistical significance (p=0.03)\n- **Key secondary endpoint met**: \"left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF)\" achieved significance (p=0.04)\n- **Study design**: Multicenter, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled with 106 DMD patients\n- **Safety profile**: \"maintained a favorable safety and tolerability profile consistent with prior clinical studies\"\n\nLate-breaking data presented at MDA 2026 showed \"additional cardiac and functional benefits.\"\n\n## Manufacturing\n\nSan Diego GMP facility is now \"operational to support potential commercial launch.\"\n\n## RMAT Designation\n\nNo specific RMAT (Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy) designation mention appears in the excerpt.\n\n## Regulatory Timeline\n\nFDA assigned a PDUFA target action date of **August 22, 2026**, with the BLA classified as a Class 2 resubmission.\n\n## HOPE-2 vs. HOPE-3 Changes\n\nNo comparative analysis between HOPE-2 and HOPE-3 protocols appears in the provided content."}]},"uuid":"af898420-8df6-4fb3-8bd2-4f0c0356e3b1","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:58:56.298Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"ee0ff6aa-f985-433d-87b7-087c0f1f8a92","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}

{"parentUuid":"2fed9df8-0031-4cf7-8e6b-e4058cb9bbd4","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"8f8acf02-923c-41cf-91db-8847a47ae6f6","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01YNLGMAGgajwGvD3APFECZy","type":"tool_result","content":"# Capricor Therapeutics 10-Q Analysis: FDA Regulatory Status\n\n## Complete Response Letter (CRL)\n\nThe document confirms Capricor received a CRL but provides **limited specific details about deficiencies**. The press release states they will \"meet with the FDA in August 2025 to discuss next steps following receipt of a Complete Response Letter (CRL) for its BLA for Deramiocel.\"\n\n## Form 483 Observations\n\n**Major milestone achieved**: \"The FDA has recently accepted all of Capricor's responses to the 483 observations noted in the PLI, reinforcing Capricor's readiness for commercial manufacturing.\"\n\nThe document does not enumerate individual 483 observations or manufacturing-specific issues at the San Diego facility.\n\n## Manufacturing & Pre-License Inspection (PLI)\n\nThe filing confirms successful resolution: \"All 483 observations noted in the Pre-License Inspection have been resolved and accepted by the FDA.\" However, **specific manufacturing deficiencies are not detailed** in this document.\n\n## RMAT Designation\n\nConfirmed in the document: \"Deramiocel has received RMAT and [additional designation]\" (text appears truncated in source material).\n\n## BLA Resubmission Timeline\n\nCompany strategy: \"Capricor seeks to resubmit the BLA based on its existing dataset, with HOPE-3 data (expected in Q4 2025) potentially serving as supportive and confirmatory evidence, pending regulatory guidance.\"\n\n## HOPE-2 Results\n\n\"Four-year results from the study showed continued preservation of cardiac function (LVEF) and slowed skeletal muscle decline (PUL v2.0).\"\n\n## HOPE-3 Design Differences\n\n\"A protocol amendment has been submitted to designate left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) as the primary efficacy endpoint\" in HOPE-3, a Phase 3 trial enrolling approximately 104 patients.\n\n**Note**: This 10-Q provides regulatory updates but lacks granular details about specific CRL deficiencies."}]},"uuid":"1253ee58-2da0-424f-9e12-f7f0e289a16b","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:59:12.377Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"2fed9df8-0031-4cf7-8e6b-e4058cb9bbd4","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}
{"parentUuid":"c32ea31e-aa2a-4043-8ff2-7669da0aea69","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"8f8acf02-923c-41cf-91db-8847a47ae6f6","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_012pX7Gekb1WUTxNmHf8GWwS","type":"tool_result","content":"# Capricor Therapeutics 8-K Analysis: August 11, 2025\n\n## Complete Response Letter (CRL)\n\nCapricor received a CRL for its Deramiocel BLA but the filing provides minimal detail about specific deficiencies. The company states it will \"resubmit the BLA based on its existing dataset\" and may supplement with HOPE-3 data pending FDA guidance. No enumerated FDA criticisms are disclosed in this document.\n\n## Form 483 Observations\n\nThe Pre-License Inspection yielded 483 observations. Significantly, the company reports: \"The FDA has recently accepted all of Capricor's responses to the 483 observations noted in the PLI.\" This represents complete resolution of manufacturing inspection findings, marking what management calls \"a major milestone in our regulatory progress and commercial manufacturing readiness.\"\n\n## Manufacturing Issues\n\nThe press release contains no discussion of specific manufacturing defects or quality failures. The successful closure of all 483 observations suggests previous GMP or operational concerns have been remedied, though the nature of those issues remains undisclosed.\n\n## Regulatory Path Forward\n\nThe company emphasizes an upcoming \"Type A meeting with U.S. FDA\" scheduled for August 2025 to \"discuss next steps\" and define an approval pathway. HOPE-3 Phase 3 data—expected Q4 2025—may serve as \"supportive and confirmatory evidence\" for BLA resubmission."}]},"uuid":"daf27a99-8654-425f-9be4-0619cfbb7a8d","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:59:12.415Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"c32ea31e-aa2a-4043-8ff2-7669da0aea69","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}

{"parentUuid":"4bd30838-e1e5-4bcf-981b-2bc718bb6d39","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"8f8acf02-923c-41cf-91db-8847a47ae6f6","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01SdoKnTXWZ6zAv8s2jyvbWz","type":"tool_result","content":"# Capricor Therapeutics 8-K Filing - December 3, 2025\n\nI've reviewed the webpage content provided, which displays the index/landing page for Capricor's 8-K filing from December 3, 2025. However, **the actual detailed content of the filing is not included in the source material** — only the webpage structure, navigation elements, and metadata are visible.\n\nThe page shows:\n- **Filing Type:** 8-K (Material Event)\n- **Date:** December 3, 2025\n- **CIK:** 1133869\n- **Events Listed:** Financial Exhibit, Regulated Disclosure\n\n## What's Missing\n\nTo extract specific details about:\n- HOPE-3 trial results\n- BLA (Biologics License Application) resubmission status\n- CRL (Complete Response Letter) resolution\n\n...I would need access to the actual filing content, particularly **Exhibit 99.1** or the main disclosure sections, which are referenced but not displayed in the provided HTML.\n\n**Recommendation:** Access the full filing directly via the SEC website (sec.gov) or the Last10K.com member portal to retrieve the substantive clinical and regulatory updates you're seeking."}]},"uuid":"cc684edd-ea39-44ad-8204-3a7244fba6ae","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:59:37.355Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"4bd30838-e1e5-4bcf-981b-2bc718bb6d39","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}
{"parentUuid":"27a3e650-c24f-4322-8421-36a4a7ce9450","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"8f8acf02-923c-41cf-91db-8847a47ae6f6","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NJuSUkjeNFKdGaGBXucEjR","type":"tool_result","content":"# Capricor Therapeutics (CAPR) November 2025 Filing: Key Regulatory Details\n\n## Complete Response Letter (CRL) Specifics\nThe filing does not detail specific deficiencies from the CRL. It only states that \"the FDA supported the submission of the HOPE-3 results in order to address the issues raised in the CRL.\"\n\n## Form 483 Observations\nThe company reported success with its Pre-License Inspection: \"All 483 observations were addressed and accepted by FDA, and the facility is now operational.\"\n\n## Manufacturing Status\nThe San Diego GMP facility achieved full operational capability. The facility \"successfully completed its FDA Pre-License Inspection (PLI)\" and now supports \"initial commercial launch upon approval, with systems for product quality, scalability, and consistency.\"\n\n## Type A Meeting Outcome\nIn August 2025, the FDA indicated the path forward: \"During our Type A meeting in August, the FDA supported the submission of the HOPE-3 results in order to address the issues raised in the CRL.\"\n\n## HOPE-3 Trial Status\nThe pivotal study completed enrollment and treatment phases. \"Capricor has completed the 12-month treatment phase of its pivotal HOPE-3 study (n=105).\" Topline results were expected \"in the coming weeks (Q4 2025).\"\n\n## Regulatory Timeline\nThe resubmission strategy involves: \"The resubmission is expected to be reviewed under a Type 2 classification with an anticipated review period of up to six months.\""}]},"uuid":"2d46d29f-75a1-420e-bf03-f0e1f1d9b221","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:59:39.059Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"27a3e650-c24f-4322-8421-36a4a7ce9450","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}

{"parentUuid":"4e4ec6a8-fe42-483b-ad9b-25ed4d984d5c","isSidechain":true,"agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_0144v1EbVjzXJ2EAxZa5by1t","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01YCmbPQyYhjXaTBPebQ5vq6","name":"WebFetch","input":{"url":"https://last10k.com/sec-filing/1133869/0001558370-25-003707.htm","prompt":"This is Capricor's March 2025 10-K annual report. Extract ALL details about: 1) The transition from HOPE-2 to HOPE-3 (what changed in design, endpoints, dosing, patient population). 2) The BLA submission and PDUFA date. 3) Advisory committee meeting. 4) RMAT designation. 5) Nippon Shinyaku partnership terms. 6) Manufacturing process description. 7) Prior failures or setbacks. 8) How they interpreted HOPE-2 results to design HOPE-3."},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":301,"cache_creation_input_tokens":626,"cache_read_input_tokens":55928,"output_tokens":257,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":626},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CZEmUoafEwXKuu3VgdN7q","type":"assistant","uuid":"70ce9747-2052-4547-b8e6-58db489e2685","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:59:46.855Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}
{"parentUuid":"70ce9747-2052-4547-b8e6-58db489e2685","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"8f8acf02-923c-41cf-91db-8847a47ae6f6","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01YCmbPQyYhjXaTBPebQ5vq6","type":"tool_result","content":"# Capricor Therapeutics: HOPE-2 to HOPE-3 Transition & Development Overview\n\n## 1) HOPE-2 to HOPE-3 Transition\n\n**Design Changes:**\nThe HOPE-2 trial was a \"Phase 2 placebo-controlled trial\" focusing on cardiac function. HOPE-3 represents a Phase 3 study with a different emphasis: it is \"assessing skeletal muscle function\" rather than primary cardiac endpoints. The company noted that \"HOPE-3 Phase 3 study...has not been requested for review by the FDA for this application,\" indicating the cardiac BLA proceeded without Phase 3 cardiac data.\n\n**Patient Population & Endpoints:**\nHOPE-2 enrolled patients with DMD cardiomyopathy and demonstrated \"statistically significant and clinically relevant improvements in cardiac function up to three-years after treatment.\" The HOPE-2 open label extension (OLE) showed \"preservation of skeletal muscle function over 3 years resulting in 52% slowing of disease.\" HOPE-3 shifts focus to measure skeletal muscle outcomes, with the document noting assessments of \"skeletal muscle function.\"\n\n## 2) BLA Submission & PDUFA Date\n\n\"The FDA accepted Capricor's BLA seeking full approval of deramiocel for the treatment of individuals with Duchenne muscular dystrophy cardiomyopathy\" in March 2025. The BLA received \"Priority Review\" with \"a Prescription Drug User Fee Act (PDUFA) action date set for August 31, 2025.\"\n\n## 3) Advisory Committee Meeting\n\nNo advisory committee meeting is mentioned in the provided document excerpts.\n\n## 4) RMAT Designation\n\nNo Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy (RMAT) designation is explicitly discussed in the materials provided.\n\n## 5) Nippon Shinyaku Partnership Terms\n\nCapricor \"received $10 million milestone payment from Nippon Shinyaku\" in 2024. The company is \"preparing for potential commercial launch with our partner Nippon Shinyaku Co. (U.S. subsidiary: NS Pharma Inc).\" Regarding distribution: \"Nippon Shinyaku would be responsible\" for commercialization activities under their distribution agreement, though specific royalty or profit-sharing percentages are not detailed in these excerpts.\n\n## 6) Manufacturing Process Description\n\nDetails are limited. The document states deramiocel \"consists of allogeneic cardiosphere-derived cells (CDCs), a rare population of cardiac cells.\" The mechanism involves cells that \"act by secreting exosomes, which reduce fibrosis in muscle.\" In February 2025, \"Capricor entered into an amendment to its current lease for additional GMP space in its headquarters located in San Diego, California to support additional commercial manufacturing capacity and throughput.\"\n\n## 7) Prior Failures or Setbacks\n\nThe provided excerpts do not detail previous clinical or regulatory failures. The discussion focuses on positive HOPE-2 outcomes and the path forward.\n\n## 8) HOPE-2 Results Informing HOPE-3 Design\n\nHOPE-2 demonstrated cardiac benefits; the 52% slowing of skeletal muscle disease in the OLE phase appears to have prompted the Phase 3 focus on skeletal muscle as a secondary benefit worth formal evaluation. However, explicit design rationale linking HOPE-2 skeletal muscle signals to HOPE-3 endpoints is not fully elaborated in these excerpts."}]},"uuid":"79eefbb8-efbd-4522-adf4-fd1e03ca7f65","timestamp":"2026-03-20T17:59:59.077Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"70ce9747-2052-4547-b8e6-58db489e2685","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}

{"parentUuid":"2ec20ade-1b37-4723-80bc-dbdca182e631","isSidechain":true,"agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01XYPfn6BuQ3AXi8rT2ebmtp","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_015cvV3oX1qGqVr4EwGDa99r","name":"WebFetch","input":{"url":"https://last10k.com/sec-filing/1133869/0001558370-24-002861.htm","prompt":"This is Capricor's March 2024 10-K. Extract ALL details about: 1) RMAT designation date and basis. 2) How HOPE-2 data was interpreted. 3) Changes from HOPE-1 to HOPE-2 (route of administration change from intracoronary to IV). 4) Nippon Shinyaku partnership timeline. 5) BLA filing strategy. 6) Manufacturing facility details. 7) Any discussion of prior setbacks, failed approaches, or design evolution across trials."},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1351,"cache_read_input_tokens":56554,"output_tokens":242,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1351},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"}},"requestId":"req_011CZEmWCrfeXPrZ4d6aZ11F","type":"assistant","uuid":"f6129971-5dc2-454c-a9c6-e8a7ce33b3c1","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:00:05.614Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}
{"parentUuid":"f6129971-5dc2-454c-a9c6-e8a7ce33b3c1","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"8f8acf02-923c-41cf-91db-8847a47ae6f6","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_015cvV3oX1qGqVr4EwGDa99r","type":"tool_result","content":"# Capricor Therapeutics (CAPR) 10-K Analysis - March 2024\n\n## 1) RMAT Designation Date and Basis\n\nThe document confirms CAP-1002 received \"Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy Designation\" but does not specify the exact date of this designation. The RMAT status is mentioned as a current designation supporting the development pathway, indicating FDA recognition of the therapeutic's potential to address serious conditions with limited alternatives.\n\n## 2) HOPE-2 Data Interpretation\n\nThe filing references positive outcomes: \"At the two-year timepoint, data [from HOPE-2]...\" without providing complete specifics in the excerpt. The document indicates the study met its primary endpoints, with plans to \"report 3-year data from HOPE-2 Open-Label Extension (OLE) Trial in Q2 2024.\"\n\n## 3) Route of Administration Changes (HOPE-1 to HOPE-2)\n\nThe document does not explicitly detail the shift from intracoronary to intravenous administration between trial phases. However, it notes the current HOPE-3 trial involves \"CAP-1002 cell therapy\" for DMD without specifying administration route changes from earlier iterations.\n\n## 4) Nippon Shinyaku Partnership Timeline\n\nThe filing states: \"Nippon Shinyaku will be responsible for...\" distribution obligations but omits the partnership execution date from this excerpt. The partnership appears established for commercialization purposes.\n\n## 5) BLA Filing Strategy\n\nThe company plans an \"upcoming Type-B Meeting with FDA in Q1 2024 to discuss commercial manufacturing planning with an aim to expedite BLA pathway for CAP-1002.\" Top-line HOPE-3 data reporting is targeted for Q4 2024, preceding formal BLA submission.\n\n## 6) Manufacturing Facility Details\n\n\"CAP-1002 scale-up expansion underway at new San Diego manufacturing facility in preparation for commercial launch.\" The facility was designed and completed in 2023, with clinical dosing of HOPE-3 Cohort B beginning from this location.\n\n## 7) Prior Setbacks and Design Evolution\n\nThe document does not discuss failed approaches or prior setbacks. It emphasizes \"robust and consistent efficacy combined with a favorable safety/tolerability profile\" across trials without detailing design modifications or abandoned strategies."}]},"uuid":"8fe38e27-71c7-4adb-ac93-f6189d53ff1d","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:00:18.189Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"f6129971-5dc2-454c-a9c6-e8a7ce33b3c1","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}

{"parentUuid":"72c51f9e-2441-4bde-8197-ec2f8258d78f","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"8f8acf02-923c-41cf-91db-8847a47ae6f6","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01QeBPPWxiCbjhgcGpHJq54b","type":"tool_result","content":"# Capricor Therapeutics CAP-1002 Program Analysis\n\nBased on the March 2022 10-K filing, here's what can be determined about CAP-1002's development trajectory:\n\n## Route of Administration Changes\nThe document does not explicitly detail the transition from intracoronary to IV delivery or explain the rationale behind this shift. The filing mentions HOPE-2 and HOPE-3 trials but doesn't compare delivery methods between them.\n\n## Trial Information\nThe 10-K confirms that \"HOPE-2 clinical trial\" was completed in 2021, achieving \"positive outcomes for critical study endpoints\" in DMD patients, with results showing reduced decline in upper limb function and improved cardiac function.\n\n## HOPE-3 Design\nHOPE-3 is described as \"the Phase 3 clinical trial...expected to be the pivotal trial for CAP-1002 in DMD,\" supported by the Nippon Shinyaku partnership. Specific dose changes or endpoint evolution details aren't provided in this document.\n\n## Nippon Shinyaku Partnership\nAnnounced in 2021, this partnership grants exclusive commercialization and distribution rights for CAP-1002 in DMD. The deal includes \"$30 million upfront payment with potential additional sales and development milestone payments of up to $705 million.\"\n\n## Missing Information\nThe filing lacks details on:\n- RMAT (Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy) designation specifics\n- Prior program failures or pivots\n- Company early history\n- Specific rationale for design decisions\n\nThe document focuses on current progress rather than historical development decisions."}]},"uuid":"160cc824-b247-40bd-a0ce-f5c63aba7d6f","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:00:33.391Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"72c51f9e-2441-4bde-8197-ec2f8258d78f","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}

{"parentUuid":"7b674ef2-dc47-45bf-8fdc-bc82af833773","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"8f8acf02-923c-41cf-91db-8847a47ae6f6","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_011bBMstQzcsGcnX3vd65NKq","type":"tool_result","content":"# Capricor Therapeutics: Clinical Program Development Analysis\n\nBased on the March 2021 10-K filing, here's what the document reveals about Capricor's therapeutic strategy:\n\n## Delivery Route Transition (HOPE-1 to HOPE-2)\n\nThe filing does not explicitly detail **why** Capricor switched from intracoronary to intravenous delivery between trials. The document mentions \"HOPE-2 trial demonstrated statistically significant results in upper limb skeletal muscle and cardiac function\" but does not explain the rationale for the route change or acknowledge HOPE-1 specifics.\n\n## HOPE-2 Trial Design Rationale\n\nThe document provides limited trial design details. It states the company is \"currently discussing this program with FDA to determine the appropriate path forward,\" suggesting ongoing dialogue about the trial's approach, but specific design rationales aren't articulated in the excerpts provided.\n\n## Failed Programs\n\nThe 10-K does not explicitly discuss failed myocardial infarction or heart failure programs. The filing focuses on current assets rather than detailing abandoned indications.\n\n## Early Company History and Pivots\n\nThe document does not contain substantial early company history or pivot details in the sections provided.\n\n## RMAT Designation\n\nNo mention of RMAT (Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy) designation appears in the available content.\n\n## Allogenal Trial\n\nThe Allogenal trial is not referenced in the provided excerpts.\n\n## Prior Cardiac Programs\n\nThe filing does not delineate cardiac-specific programs preceding the DMD focus.\n\n## DMD vs. Cardiac Decision\n\nThe document doesn't explain the strategic decision-making process for prioritizing Duchenne muscular dystrophy over cardiac indications.\n\n**Note:** The web page excerpt provided appears to be a summary/landing page rather than the complete 10-K text, limiting detailed analysis."}]},"uuid":"86253107-9789-4605-b60e-0948305662de","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:00:47.126Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"7b674ef2-dc47-45bf-8fdc-bc82af833773","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}

{"parentUuid":"7e6340a9-84cc-41fa-ae49-efeb16abd60e","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"8f8acf02-923c-41cf-91db-8847a47ae6f6","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01W6cK9Q7APsaipUYyWpTNMk","type":"tool_result","content":"# Capricor Therapeutics: Clinical Programs & Company History (March 2018 10-K)\n\n## ALLSTAR Trial (Myocardial Infarction)\nThe document mentions that in Q4 2017, Capricor \"notified CIRM of its election to abandon the ALLSTAR (CIRM-funded) project\" and entered into an amendment whereby \"the total loan balance was forgiven by CIRM.\" The company classified this loan forgiveness—approximately $15.7 million—as non-cash income. However, the filing provides no details about trial results, design, or why abandonment occurred.\n\n## DYNAMIC Trial\nNo information about a DYNAMIC trial appears in this document.\n\n## Failed Clinical Programs\nBeyond ALLSTAR's abandonment, the filing does not explicitly detail other failed programs. It mentions an \"impairment of in-process research and development\" of $1.5 million in 2016 but provides no specifics on which programs were affected.\n\n## HOPE-1 Intracoronary Delivery Rationale\nThe filing does not explain why intracoronary delivery was selected for HOPE-1. It only states that HOPE-Duchenne \"found that a single intracoronary dose of CAP-1002 produced significant and sustained improvement in cardiac and skeletal muscle functions.\"\n\n## Decision to Pursue DMD\nNo explanation for pivoting to Duchenne muscular dystrophy appears in this excerpt.\n\n## HOPE-2 Design & IV Delivery Rationale\nThe document describes HOPE-2 as \"a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study in later stage Duchenne muscular dystrophy patients\" designed for \"non-ambulant\" subjects. However, it provides no rationale for switching delivery routes or methodology evolution.\n\n## Company History & Pivots\nThe filing offers minimal historical context. It mentions Cedars-Sinai \"Exclusive License Agreements\" for cardiosphere-derived cells but supplies no founding narrative or prior strategic shifts.\n\n## Eduardo Marban & CDC Origins\nMarban is not mentioned in this document. The origin of cardiosphere-derived cells is attributed to \"Cedars-Sinai Medical Center\" through license agreements, with no founder attribution provided.\n\n## Prior Setbacks or Regulatory Issues\nNone are disclosed beyond the ALLSTAR abandonment. The company received FDA clearance for HOPE-2's IND application and secured \"Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy (RMAT)\" and \"Rare Pediatric Disease\" designations—suggesting regulatory progress rather than setbacks."}]},"uuid":"fc92f519-17bd-49a1-b005-f5e19d19955f","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:01:02.948Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"7e6340a9-84cc-41fa-ae49-efeb16abd60e","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}

{"parentUuid":"77e4edbd-a2b3-445b-8d31-044d2a927fb6","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"8f8acf02-923c-41cf-91db-8847a47ae6f6","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KZRkLtPdgUKBevugqdy6y4","type":"tool_result","content":"# ALLSTAR Trial Summary\n\n**NCT Number:** NCT01458405\n\n**Title:** \"Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Phase I/II Study of the Safety and Efficacy of Intracoronary Delivery of Allogeneic Cardiosphere-Derived Cells in Patients With a Myocardial Infarction and Ischemic Left Ventricular Dysfunction\"\n\n**Phase:** Phase I/II\n\n**Status:** Terminated (February 28, 2019)\n\n**Primary Endpoints:**\n- Safety events within one month post-infusion, including myocarditis, sudden death, and major adverse cardiac events\n- Percent change in infarct size at 12 months by MRI imaging\n\n**Key Results:**\nThe study showed no significant difference between CAP-1002 and placebo groups. At month 12, infarct size improved by 7.11% (placebo) versus 8.80% (treatment), with p-value of 0.6453.\n\n**Termination Reason:**\n\"On February 28, 2019, Capricor ceased ongoing follow-up activities and terminated the ALLSTAR trial to focus resourcing on its active CAP-1002 program, HOPE-2.\"\n\nThe sponsor discontinued the trial after determining CAP-1002 would not be developed further for this particular indication, shifting resources to an alternative study."}]},"uuid":"7f07bef5-2b63-455e-91d4-cf35f39abbf0","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:01:15.636Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"77e4edbd-a2b3-445b-8d31-044d2a927fb6","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}
{"parentUuid":"54356b6c-d4f4-4403-bdf9-b91316a6b1b6","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"8f8acf02-923c-41cf-91db-8847a47ae6f6","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01LYURoZ4rkNuKWP1KjmfY72","type":"tool_result","content":"I appreciate your detailed request, but I need to clarify an important issue: **The document provided is Isoray, Inc.'s 10-K filing from September 2016, not Capricor's filing.**\n\nIsoray is a medical technology company focused on seed brachytherapy and radioisotope applications for cancer treatment (prostate, brain, lung, head/neck, and gynecological cancers). The document contains:\n\n- Financial results for fiscal year ending June 30, 2016\n- Revenue of $4.77 million (4% increase year-over-year)\n- Operating loss of $5.08 million\n- Cash position of $15.4 million with no debt\n\n**None of the information you requested appears in this document:**\n- No ALLSTAR trial details\n- No DYNAMIC trial information\n- No Eduardo Marban references\n- No cardiosphere-derived cells technology\n- No cardiac or DMD programs\n- No intracoronary delivery discussions\n\nThese topics relate to Capricor Therapeutics, a different company focused on regenerative medicine and cell therapy for cardiac and muscular dystrophy indications.\n\n**To obtain the information you seek, you would need Capricor's September 2016 10-K filing**, not this Isoray document. {"parentUuid":"b729878c-2cab-4626-9683-e81bdec3d5fa","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"8f8acf02-923c-41cf-91db-8847a47ae6f6","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01YYBJapcqVFtc7TVF6mbhYv","type":"tool_result","content":"# ALLSTAR Trial Publications\n\nBased on the PubMed search results, here are the 4 publications about the ALLSTAR trial:\n\n## 1. **Effect of cardiosphere-derived cells on segmental myocardial function after myocardial infarction: ALLSTAR randomised clinical trial**\n- **Authors:** Ostovaneh MR, Makkar RR, Ambale-Venkatesh B, et al.\n- **Journal:** Open Heart\n- **Date:** July 2021\n- **PMID:** 34233913\n- **Focus:** Examines segmental myocardial function improvements, addressing limitations of global left ventricular function measures\n\n## 2. **Intracoronary ALLogeneic heart STem cells to Achieve myocardial Regeneration (ALLSTAR): a randomized, placebo-controlled, double-blinded trial**\n- **Authors:** Makkar RR, Kereiakes DJ, Aguirre F, et al.\n- **Journal:** European Heart Journal\n- **Date:** September 2020\n- **PMID:** 32749459\n- **Focus:** Primary trial results testing cardiosphere-derived cells in acute myocardial infarction\n\n## 3. **ALLogeneic Heart STem Cells to Achieve Myocardial Regeneration (ALLSTAR) Trial: Rationale and Design**\n- **Authors:** Chakravarty T, Makkar RR, Ascheim DD, et al.\n- **Journal:** Cell Transplant\n- **Date:** February 2017\n- **PMID:** 27543900\n- **Focus:** Trial protocol and design rationale\n\n## 4. **Enhancing retention and efficacy of cardiosphere-derived cells administered after myocardial infarction using a hyaluronan-gelatin hydrogel**\n- **Authors:** Smith RR, Marbán E, Marbán L\n- **Journal:** Biomatter\n- **Date:** January 2013\n- **PMID:** 23538511\n- **Focus:** Supporting research on cell delivery optimization"}]},"uuid":"d6478ee0-6c91-4317-9659-02414e989570","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:01:33.243Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"b729878c-2cab-4626-9683-e81bdec3d5fa","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}

{"parentUuid":"b3a9a371-39fa-4589-bc79-dabd84e4e244","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"8f8acf02-923c-41cf-91db-8847a47ae6f6","agentId":"a3c1f306c9b92610e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KQBKVRf2MCNv8ss6Mo7AWu","type":"tool_result","content":"# ALLSTAR Trial Summary\n\n## Study Design\nA multicenter, randomized, double-blinded, placebo-controlled trial evaluating intracoronary allogeneic cardiosphere-derived cells (CDCs) in post-MI patients. Patients were enrolled \"4 weeks to 12 months after MI, with left ventricular ejection fraction (LVEF) ≤45%.\"\n\n## Patient Population\n142 eligible patients enrolled; 134 treated (90 CDC group, 44 placebo). Mean baseline LVEF was 40% with mean scar size of 22% of LV mass.\n\n## Primary Efficacy Endpoint\n\"The relative percentage change in infarct size at 12 months post-infusion as assessed by contrast-enhanced cardiac MRI.\" However, the trial was halted early at interim 6-month analysis.\n\n## Key Results\n- **Scar size change (6 months):** No difference between groups (P = 0.51)\n- **LV end-diastolic volume:** Significant reduction with CDC treatment (P = 0.02)\n- **LV end-systolic volume:** Significant reduction with CDC treatment (P = 0.02)\n- **NT-proBNP:** Significant reduction with CDC treatment (P = 0.02)\n\n## Trial Discontinuation\nThe trial was \"subsequently stopped due to the low probability of detecting a significant treatment effect of CDCs based on the primary endpoint.\"\n\n## Conclusions\n\"Intracoronary infusion of allogeneic CDCs in patients with post-MI LV dysfunction was safe but did not reduce scar size relative to placebo at 6 months.\" However, improvements in LV volumes and biomarkers suggested disease-modifying activity."}]},"uuid":"ab51888c-95af-449e-9176-4b3c4c5d03c3","timestamp":"2026-03-20T18:01:46.575Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"b3a9a371-39fa-4589-bc79-dabd84e4e244","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/junekim/Documents/june.kim","sessionId":"6d5431e1-97f0-46be-9bdf-36e96721569f","version":"2.1.79","gitBranch":"master","slug":"modular-sparking-forest"}
